title: tragopogon crocifolius subsp. samaritani
intro: Tragopogon crocifolius subsp. samaritani, also known as Samaritan salsify or Samaritan goat's beard, is a plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Tragopogon crocifolius subsp. samaritani include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the roots or leaves in hot water or oil, and are used to treat a range of health issues.
